content:
# Diffusion Coupling

**Summary**: The degree to which different pore systems are connected and allow fluid movement between them.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-al-duailej|Al-Duailej, 2014]] evaluated diffusion coupling using NMR to determine the connectivity of micro- and macro-pores.

- [[2023-al-boghail|AL-BOGHAIL, 2023]] investigated diffusion coupling in carbonate formations under multiple fluid conditions.

## Scientific Context

- [[2014-al-duailej|Al-Duailej, 2014]] High diffusion coupling indicates a well-connected pore system.

- [[2023-al-boghail|AL-BOGHAIL, 2023]] investigated diffusion coupling in carbonate formations under multiple fluid conditions.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2014-al-duailej|Al-Duailej, 2014]] determined by the overlap of NMR peaks.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Emulsified acid and Chelating Agent II showed an increase in the interconnectivities between the two porosities. ([[2014-al-duailej|Al-Duailej, 2014, p. 110]])


- Diffusion coupling leads to inaccurate petrophysical properties like irreducible water saturation and permeability when it is not accounted for. ([[2023-al-boghail|AL-BOGHAIL, 2023, p. 28]])
